records.stage_evidence: PTCA procedure notes detailing stent deployment and balloon inflation

ocr.verbatim:
PCI REPORT

Name : Age : 74 years
Sex : Male
Date : 03/04/2026
Ref By :

PTCA to RCA (OT Notes)
Procedure done under local anesthesia
Route: Rt Femoral
Total Heparin used: 6000 U
JR was cannulated with 6F JR 3.5 guiding catheter and diagnostic shoots taken
in various views showed RCA lesion.

1) The Lesion was crossed with Rinato wire and predilatation done with 2.5X12 mm
predilatation balloon at 18atms for 20 secs

2) Supra Explorer 3.0X48 mm stent (DES) was then deployed in Mid RCA at 16 atms
for 30 secs and check shoots repeated .

3) Overlapping Supra Explorer 3.5X20 mm stent ( DES) was then deployed at 16 atms
for 20 secs

4) Post dilatation was done with 3.5 x 12 mm N.C. balloon at 20 atms for 10 secs

5) There was no dissection. An adequate deployed stent with distal TIMI III flow was
seen.

No peri-procedural complications

Sahajanand Medical Technologies Limited
SUPRA EXPLORER
3.00 mm x 48 mm
HEF FGTX300048
SN (21)P25TXAEGS300048085
LOT (10)P25TXAEGAB
B (17)2027-04

Sahajanand Medical Technologies Limited
SUPRA EXPLORER
3 50 mm x 20 mm
HEF FGTX350020
SN (21)S25TXAYOS350020081
LOT (10)S25TXAYOA
B (17)2027-09

Scanned with OKEN Scanner

records.summary: This is a typed PCI (Percutaneous Coronary Intervention) report detailing a procedure performed on a 74-year-old male patient on April 3, 2026. The report describes the step-by-step process of PTCA (Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty) to the Right Coronary Artery (RCA), including the use of a guiding catheter, predilatation, deployment of two overlapping drug-eluting stents (DES), post-dilatation, and confirmation of adequate stent deployment with TIMI III flow. No complications were noted.
key_observations: Procedure performed on a 74-year-old male on April 3, 2026.
PTCA to RCA via right femoral route under local anesthesia.
Two drug-eluting stents (Supra Explorer 3.0x48 mm and 3.5x20 mm) deployed sequentially.
Post-dilatation performed with a 3.5x12 mm balloon.
Successful outcome: adequate stent deployment with TIMI III flow, no dissection or complications.
Stent details and serial numbers provided for both devices.

ocr.verbatim:
CORONARY ANGIOGRAPHY REPORT
Patient Name : 
Cath ID No. : SSVH/268/26
Ref. Dr. : 
Age/Sex : 75Yrs / M
Pt. Reg. No.: D-37/2026
Date : 01/04/2026

LCA
RCA

HISTORY : Chest Pain.
LMCA : Normal.
LAD : Osteoproximal 50% stenosis.
DIAGONAL : Small caliber, but long vessel.
RI : Mid segment has 50% stenosis in small caliber
long vessel.
LCx : Non-Dominant & Normal.
OM : Normal.
RCA : Dominant & has long segment 85-90% stenosis involving
proximal to mid segments. Rest normal.
PDA / PLV : Normal.
IMPRESSION : Critical stenosis of RCA.
ADVICE : PTCA + Stents (2) in RCA.

DNB (Medicine), DNB (Cardio' xy)

Scanned with OKEN Scanner

records.summary: This is a post-procedure coronary angiography report for a 75-year-old male patient with chest pain, showing critical stenosis in the right coronary artery (RCA) and recommending PTCA with two stents. The left coronary artery (LCA) branches appear normal or with mild stenosis.
key_observations: Patient age: 75 years, male
Critical stenosis (85-90%) in RCA proximal to mid segments
Advised intervention: PTCA + 2 stents in RCA
Normal findings in LMCA, LCx, OM, PDA/PLV
History of chest pain
Report date: 01/04/2026
